allies.rationale_text: Experimentation helps optionality buy learning cheaply before the irreversible choice is forced.
allies.source_quote: Most useful when uncertainty is high, early commitment is expensive, and cheap experiments can buy learning before an irreversible call.

allies.rationale_text: Trade-offs helps optionality compare multiple paths by making the downside and sacrificed alternatives explicit instead of binary.
allies.source_quote: By generating numerous options, this mental model aims to make decision-making more effective and helps compare the upside potential against the risk of the downside potential.

allies.rationale_text: Decision-trees help optionality model branching choices and probabilistic outcomes instead of treating the future as one track.
allies.source_quote: decision trees can be used, particularly if some choices are probabilistic in nature

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias shrinks optionality by locking attention onto the preferred path and filtering out superior alternatives.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation bias causes us to seek information that confirms an initial belief or preferred option, leading to an inability to see superior alternatives.

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-and-consistency-bias makes optionality psychologically expensive because prior choices become harder to revisit even when better paths appear.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Optionality conflicts with commitment-bias when preserving options delays the commitment needed to capture time-sensitive value.
structured_tensions.source_quote: optionality conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when preserving options delays the commitment needed to capture time-sensitive value.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Optionality conflicts with prioritization when keeping all options open prevents the ruthless elimination that focused execution requires.
structured_tensions.source_quote: optionality conflicts with prioritization (prioritization) when keeping all options open prevents the ruthless elimination that focused execution requires.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on ally and antagonist logic rather than on an explicit tension block, so the file preserves that smaller first-order surface.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation inflation where the raw source did not provide an explicit structured tension block.
open_questions: Systems-thinking is a source-real ally, but experimentation, trade-offs, and decision-trees were the cleaner bounded links into the current graph.
